title: Content Console Usage Guide
description: Covers the content types, list search, edit preview, and download/delete capabilities of the astro-whono local Content Console in the development environment.
text: astro-whono ships a local Content Console for managing the site's written content during development. The Content Console entry point is . It covers browsing, searching, editing, and previewing four content types — essays, bits, memo, and about — and supports creating drafts, downloading source files, and deleting, so you can maintain content without hand-writing frontmatter. :::note[Development only] and its editor pages are only operable in the development environment. In production they show a local-dev notice and load no content data or editor; only serves the local back office and is not

title: Theme Console Configuration Guide
description: Covers the scope, page groups, storage targets, and save mechanism of the astro-whono local Theme Console in the development environment.
text: astro-whono ships a local Theme Console for managing theme-level configuration in one place during development. The Theme Console entry point is . It mainly covers site info, the sidebar, the home page, inner-page copy, and a few reading and code-display options, so you can quickly tune the theme settings after forking or cloning. :::note[Development only] is only writable in the development environment. In production it shows a local-dev notice and offers no write access. ::: Local startup and entry point Start the project locally with: By default the dev server runs at . Once it is up, open:

title: Admin Console Quick Guide
description: Introduces the entry points and per-page features of the astro-whono local Admin Console.
text: Last test image. The Admin Console at is the local back-office entry point, used to take over site configuration and content maintenance after forking, cloning, or self-hosting. It is not a standalone CMS. Save operations write back to configuration or content files inside the repository, so it pairs well with Git: you can review diffs before and after changes, and roll back like any normal project file when needed. :::note[Local tool] The Admin Console only offers write access in the development environment. In production it keeps at most a read-only site overview page; only serves the local

title: Markdown Formatting Guide
description: Demonstrates every Markdown format effect the theme supports, including headings, lists, code, tables, quotes, and more.
text: This article demonstrates every Markdown formatting effect supported by the theme. First paragraph… (used for list preview) Continued body… Text formatting This is plain text. **This is bold**, *this is italic*, and ***this is bold italic***. You can also use ~~strikethrough~~ to mark deprecated content. Inline code is wrapped in backticks: — handy for marking variable names or commands. Quotes The value of design does not end when something is built. Good design should stand the test of time, retaining its unique appeal and usefulness as the years go by. You can also use multi-paragraph quote

title: Building a Modern Web Application with Astro
description: A comprehensive guide to creating fast, content-focused websites
text: Astro is a modern web framework built for content-heavy sites that need strong performance without giving up developer ergonomics. Why Astro? In the ever-evolving landscape of web development, choosing the right framework can make or break your project. Astro stands out for several compelling reasons: **Performance First** — Ship less JavaScript and keep the default runtime light **Content Focused** — A strong fit for blogs, docs, landing pages, and design-heavy sites **Flexible** — Use Astro components first, and add React, Vue, Svelte, or other UI islands only where they help **Composable Re

title: Code Highlighting Test
description: Tests syntax highlighting across different programming languages.
text: This article tests the theme's code-highlighting support across various programming languages. TypeScript Rust Go SQL JSON YAML Code highlighting uses Shiki and supports 100+ programming languages.
